name: lint_code
description: Analyzes source code for style violations, potential bugs, and code quality issues using language-appropriate linting rules. Optionally applies auto-fixes for correctable issues.

code.description: The source code string to lint and analyze.
examples:
const unused = 42;
function greet(name) { console.log('Hello ' + name) }
code.$comment: Code is linted in-memory; no file system access required. Large files may be truncated at backend's max token limit.

language.description: The programming language of the submitted code. Determines which linter and rule set to apply.
language.$comment: Enum restricted to commonly used languages with stable, widely-adopted linters (pylint/flake8, ESLint, golangci-lint, clippy, etc.).

rules.description: Optional list of specific lint rule IDs or rule categories to enforce. When omitted, the default rule set for the language is applied.
rules.$comment: Rule IDs are language/linter-specific. Using an unknown rule ID will be silently ignored or return an error depending on backend configuration.

fix.description: Whether to automatically apply safe, non-destructive fixes to the code and return the corrected version.
fix.$comment: Only applies fixes that are guaranteed safe (e.g., whitespace, imports). Structural refactors are never auto-applied.
